cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question. The user is asking what the International Olympic Committee (IOC) was concerned about regarding the 2008 Olympic medals that incorporated jade. The options are (A) the medals will be worth less, (B) the medals are too difficult to make, (C) competitors don't like the design, and (D) jade is fragile and easy to break up. First, I'll need to find the relevant part of the text. The passage mentions that experts from the IOC were concerned about the risk of fragility when the jade ring design was proposed. It also states that the IOC required the jade rings to be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test. The Shanghai Mint even raised the standard to 3 meters. The result was that the medals could survive a 3-meter drop with only minor cracks on the jade rings. So, the main concern from the IOC was about the fragility of the jade. They wanted to ensure that the jade rings wouldn't break easily. This directly relates to option D: "jade is fragile and easy to break up." Option A talks about the medals being worth less, but the text doesn't mention anything about the monetary value.
